compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
Discovered fixing snide-cut-mussel (index.lock resilience). The shared
`gitutil.run_git_write` now self-heals a stale/contended `index.lock` for the event-append
path (`event_append.py`) and the transition/claim path (`txn.py`). But several OTHER store
write paths still call `run_git`/their own `_git` directly for `add`/`commit` and are NOT yet
routed through `run_git_write`, so they can still fail hard on index.lock contention:
`_commands/compact.py`, `_commands/delete.py`, `_commands/init.py`, `_store/push.py`,
`_store/sync.py`.

## Expected vs Actual
- **Expected:** every ticket-store git write self-heals a stale/contended index.lock uniformly.
- **Actual:** only event-append + transition/claim are covered; compact/delete/init/push/sync
  can still surface a hard "git commit failed while holding lock" on contention.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Route the add/commit git ops in compact.py, delete.py, init.py (and evaluate push.py/sync.py)
      through `gitutil.run_git_write` so they self-heal index.lock the same way.
- [ ] A regression test per newly-covered path (or a shared parametrized one) asserting a stale
      lock is reclaimed and a contended one is ridden out.
compiled_state.title: route remaining store write paths (compact/delete/init/push/sync) through the index.lock-resilient run_git_write
